items.title: Parents accused of starving daughter victims of 'unfair trial', court rules

children.content: The WA Court of Appeal has overturned the guilty verdicts of Perth parents who starved their daughter after ruling that the judge's conduct in the jury trial amounted to a "miscarriage of justice".

gallery.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.

thumbnail.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.

items.title: 'Not about mass surveillance': WA Police first in country to use live facial recognition

children.content: WA Police are set to become the first in the country to trial live facial recognition of crowds, describing the technology as safer than standard CCTV cameras.

image.altText: A close-up shot of two live facial recognition cameras on top of a pole.

items.title: WA government wants federal help with Collie's coal transition

children.content: The state government has already tipped $700 million into the transition of the WA town of Collie away from coal. Now, the Cook government wants tens of millions of federal dollars to finish the job.

image.altText: sEVERAL COAL WORKERS HOLDING BLACK SIGNS AND A WHITE BANNER PROTESTING THE COAL TRANSITION

items.title: Suspected H5 bird flu detection in Western Australia

children.content: A wild migratory bird in Western Australia has returned a suspected positive result for avian influenza, which has devastated populations of seabirds, seals and other animals across the world.

gallery.altText: A black bird with white on its wings lies dead in the snow.

thumbnail.altText: A black bird with white on its wings lies dead in the snow.

items.title: Wife of AFL legend Tony Modra thanks first responders 'who saved his life'

children.content: The wife of AFL icon Tony Modra has expressed her gratitude to emergency services and hospital staff as the Crows legend remains in hospital in a "critical but stable condition" following a truck crash in South Australia.

items.title: WA mum warns changes to NDIS could take choices away from her son

children.content: Annette Nicholas and her husband last year became disability support workers to share their "lived experience" of raising 15-year-old son Tre, who is intellectually disabled and also lives with autism.

image.altText: A mother and son walking in a park.

items.title: Government says possum bridges successful, but sceptics question data

children.content: The WA government has released night-vision images showing scores of native wildlife are using rope bridges and underpasses along a $1.46 billion highway in the South West region, but not everyone is convinced.

gallery.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.

items.title: Dockers down Cats to stay top as Bolton takes mark of the year contender

children.content: Fremantle extends its club record AFL winning streak to 13 games, coming from 28 points down to defeat Geelong by nine points in a blockbuster clash in Perth, with Shai Bolton soaring to pull in a one-handed screamer.

articles.title: Battle-worn Freo fans dare to dream a maiden premiership is within reach

children.content: Hard-bitten Dockers supporters won't want to say it out loud, but as Fremantle flexes more muscle with each passing week, hope is slowly turning to expectation that a first AFL flag could be on the cards.

image.altText: Dockers sing their club song after winning the AFL Round 9 match between the Fremantle Dockers and the Hawthorn Hawks

gallery.altText: A Fremantle AFL forward flies high and grabs the ball in mid-air, standing on a teammate and a Geelong player.

thumbnail.altText: A Fremantle AFL forward flies high and grabs the ball in mid-air, standing on a teammate and a Geelong player.

items.title: North Queensland reacts to Rinehart's idea of giving islands to Musk

children.content: The Townsville Enterprise chief executive says "all ideas are welcome" after mining magnate Gina Rinehart suggested islands off North Queensland's coast should be offered to SpaceX CEO Elon Musk for satellite launches.

image.altText: A beach surrounding by hills and pure white sand swirling into the ocean

items.title: Albanese government finds itself in policy quicksand on three core issues

children.content: The government's announced changes to its tax package are seeking to allay the fierce post-budget backlash from vested interests and some commentators.

items.title: Hantavirus ship passenger recounts 'huge shock' as quarantine nears end

children.content: The first Australian to speak publicly about being onboard the MV Hondius shares his experience of the deadly outbreak on the cruise ship, as he prepares to leave a Perth quarantine facility and return home to his family.

gallery.altText: An elderly man in personal protection gear stands in the lobby of a cruise ship with a map on the wall
A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius

thumbnail.altText: An elderly man in personal protection gear stands in the lobby of a cruise ship with a map on the wall
A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius

items.title: The pros and cons of retiring overseas with your pension

children.content: With some foreign countries offering cheaper living expenses, it is no surprise that moving overseas to retire is increasingly attractive to some older Australians. But before you up stumps and book that flight, read this.

image.altText: Older man looks out onto a beach.

items.title: Where and when to watch Australia versus the US

children.content: The Socceroos will face the USA in their second Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's what time you'll need to set your alarm.

image.altText: Connor Metcalfe and Nishan Velupillay hug on the pitch.

items.title: Fluke, or are the Socceroos actually good enough to win against the US?

children.content: Commentators are writing off Australia in Saturday's early morning clash against the US.
Was the Socceroos' win against Türkiye a fluke or was the way the game went part of coach Tony Popovic's plan?
We take a closer look at whether Australia is being judged on the wrong metrics and why this team has fans excited.

image.altText: A snarling Patrick Beach with his gloved hand held in a tight fist. Text: A guide to the Socceroos and their play style

gallery.altText: A snarling Patrick Beach with his gloved hand held in a tight fist. Text: A guide to the Socceroos and their play style

thumbnail.altText: A snarling Patrick Beach with his gloved hand held in a tight fist. Text: A guide to the Socceroos and their play style

items.title: An orange bulldozer is ploughing through the political arena

children.content: Four years ago, Australians decided they didn't want a bulldozer for a prime minister. But now if Gina Rinehart has her way, a big orange one will be ploughing her way through the political arena.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Soccer celebrations, bin battles, phone fails and an internet-famous duck

children.content: Today's news quiz is heating up like El Niño. Will you strut like an internet-famous duck or get schooled like Prince George?

image.altText: A duck pictured wearing a Mexican football jersey

items.title: The whipping tree went from a place of pain to one of healing

children.content: In Western Australia's far north there is a "whipping tree" which for decades stood as a reminder of painful times, but has now become a place of healing and reconciliation.

gallery.altText: group of Indigenous people gathering around a table holding tree stumps

thumbnail.altText: group of Indigenous people gathering around a table holding tree stumps

items.title: Pensioner living in car has 'lost faith' in system as he waits for house

children.content: Despite upcoming changes from the WA government to overhaul the housing waitlist, a 68-year-old living in his car says he has lost faith in the system as an advocate calls for faster action.

image.altText: Mark Unsworth in car looks to camera.

items.title: New data shines light on state of EV public charger network

children.content: It's often claimed Australia has an EV public charger shortage, but analysis of exclusive data shows most fast charger sites are not congested.

image.altText: EV charging site with empty bays.

items.title: Love of film makes for a perfect mix for this WA farmer

children.content: Farmer Brianna Jones has worked on some of Western Australia's biggest film productions, such as Blueback, Sweet As, and The Surfer.

gallery.altText: a female standing in front of a tractor in the Great Southern of WA.

thumbnail.altText: a female standing in front of a tractor in the Great Southern of WA.
